text: Chat GPT just got literally alive. Not metaphorically. While you sleep, it's researching, planning, and preparing personalized updates for your morning. Open AAI just launched Chat GPD Pulse, and Sam Altman himself called it his favorite feature so far. Yeah, it's that big. For the longest time, interacting with AI meant you doing the work. You had to know what to ask, when to ask it, and hope you didn't forget to follow up. It was on you. But Pulse, Pulse flips the script completely. Here's where it gets crazy. Chat GPD now looks at your chat history, your calendar, your emails, basically everything you're doing. And by the time you wake up, you've got 5 to 10 visual cards waiting for you. It'll draft your meeting agendas, remind you of birthdays, and even suggest gifts, and recommend restaurants for your trip next week. OpenAI tested chat GPT pulse with college students, and the results are absolutely insane. One student casually mentioned a Taiwan grand trip. And the next morning, Pulse had already mapped out train schedules, commute times, and all kinds of info the student never even thought to look for. They literally said, "It exposed me to information I would have never looked for." Here's another wild one. A student struggling with scuba diving lessons and also studying marketing. Pulse created this crazy analogy connecting underwater breathing techniques to customer retention strategies. Who even thinks of that? But suddenly marketing concepts clicked through the lens of diving. It was like pulse opened new doors of understanding. It's creating analogies they never would have thought of. We're not just asking questions anymore. AI is answering them before we even think to ask. But here's the catch. This feature is not cheap. You can't just get it unless you're $200 per month pro user. Just days before Pulse launched, Sam Altman dropped this bombshell. In the next few weeks, we're rolling out some new supercomputheavy features because they're so resource inensive. They're only going to be available to pro users at first. And now we know why. They've introduced Vibes, a new inside the Meta AI app and short form AI generated videos. Basically, think Tik Tok or Instagram reels, but every video is AI slop. Well, let's just say the internet is not impressed. Mark Zuckerberg posted about Vibes on Instagram and the reaction was brutal. One of the top comments was like, "Gang, nobody wants this." No humanmade videos here. You can bring your ideas to life with AI tools, remix existing content, add music, change styles. Everything is synthetic. You can't even upload your own videos. Everything has to be AI generated from scratch or you remix stuff that's already AI made. Then you can cross-ost to Instagram reels or Facebook stories. But that's about it. Now the dots are connecting. Remember when Meta announced its partnership with Midjourney back in August? Everyone was curious why Zuckerberg chose to license their aesthetic technology. Well, turns out this was the plan all along. Meta was secretly building Vibes and needed MidJourneys visual magic to make it happen. But here's the thing. YouTube's already ahead of the game. They've started integrating Google Deep Minds VO three into YouTube shorts. You can create video backgrounds or clips with sound for free. The big difference, YouTube's integrating AI into existing tools, not launching a whole new feed full of AI generated stuff. It's AI enhancing human creativity, not just spitting out random videos. For every person calling it slob, I bet there's five who'll be addicted to scrolling through it within a few years. And honestly, I get it. It's addictive. Oh, and Elon Musk's new Vine 2.0 Zero might throw its hat in the ring with a similar AI powered feature soon. Looks like Meta beat them to the punch. We're witnessing the birth of synthetic social media where humans are optional and the content never stops. The real question is, will we even be able to tell the difference anymore? Meet Mix Board, an experimental AI powered concepting board designed to help you explore, expand, and refine your ideas faster than ever. It's Google Lab's latest experiment that's going to completely change how we think about visual creation. You can try it yourself at labs.google/mixboard powered by nanobanana, Google's next level AI image editor. So, let's say you're working on an upcycling project. You want some floral embroidery patterns for jeans. Just tell Mixboard, I'm upycling jeans. Show me seven floral embroidery patterns. And bam, within seconds, it's generating multiple visual ideas for you. Then you can drag those patterns onto your canvas and start refining. Need more inspiration? Just ask for it. Pull in the core item you're working with. Here's where the magic happens. Tell Mixboard to add all three patterns to my jeans and it instantly creates the final product with your custom designs. Want to see it in action? Ask Mixboard to show the item being worn and you get a full visual of your idea. Now, you're not just imagining the final result, you're seeing it come to life. And Mixboard isn't just for crafting. Whether you're planning an autumn party, or designing an office space in Japani style, Mixboard gives you an instant visual mood board to spark your creativity, feeling adventurous, want to see Memphis style dinner wear, or maybe some cute nano bananas, or even getting your puppy ready for Halloween? Mixboard is your playground for all ideas, big, small, or silly. Unlike Pinterest, which just curates existing content, Mixboard creates completely new visuals from scratch. And right now it's in public beta in the US through Google Labs. What will you imagine with Mixport? Now the education game changer. Learn your way. This is Google flipping textbooks upside down. Basically giving you a second AI brain to pick from right inside Microsoft 365. So, if you're dealing with giant docs or heavy research, it can process them without any hassle. Perplexity has decided to make your task easier by rolling out two big features and kicking things off with the launch of its new email assistant. This tool integrates with Gmail and Outlook to handle everyday tasks like drafting replies, scheduling meetings, and organizing emails. While this sounds like a huge help for day-to-day work, it naturally raises concerns about private information. But users don't need to worry as Perplexity has built the assistant to streamline email management while ensuring data privacy and compliance with SOC2 and GDPR standards which are the best global safety checks to make sure your emails stay private and are handled only with your permission. Alongside the email assistant, they have also introduced the Perplexity Search API, giving developers a way to tap into an enormous pool of web pages covering hundreds of billions of sources to deliver quick and smart AI powered search results in real time. It even lets you pull out precise snippets instead of wading through entire pages and deliver structured answers that make integrating it into apps much easier. China just unleashed massive AI breakthroughs in one week that have Silicon Valley scrambling. They're doing it cheaper, faster, and they're open-sourcing everything. First, Quen 3 Max Instruct. It's Alibaba's most powerful AI model. Think of it as China's answer to GPT, except it has over a trillion parameters. Basically, the building blocks that make AI smart. And here's the crazy part. On S. Bench verified, a test that measures how well AI can write actual code, it scored 69.6, putting it right up there with the best models in the world. On Tao 2 Bench, another tough AI test. It hit 74.8, actually beating both Claude Opus 4 and Deepseek 5 3.1. Second, Alibaba previewed one 2.5 models, including video generation that natively supports highfidelity audio generation, doubling the duration from 5 to 10 seconds. Quen 2.5 handles text, images, video, and audio all in one model. Something OpenAI's Sora still can't do as its video only. This puts it in direct competition with Google's V3, but arguably ahead. They also released one 2.2 Animate, a new digital human video generation model optimized for character animation and replacement. It accurately captures facial expressions and body movements of the reference character and applies them to new videos. Third, multimodal AI that understands everything. Gwen 3: Omni is a true all-in-one AI model that handles text, images, audio, and video simultaneously. Think of it as a universal translator and assistant. You could show it a foreign movie clip, and it'll understand the video, translate the dialogue, read any text on screen, and explain everything happening all at once. It supports 119 languages for text, 19 for voice input, and can speak back to you in 10 languages. This one AI handles it all without switching between different apps. Fourth, vision AI that controls computers. Quen 3 VL's visual agent capability allows the model to operate computer and mobile graphical user interfaces. The flagship is a massive 235 billion parameter model made freely available to developers worldwide. The AI community is losing it. This is all part of a massive push.
